Nabu-išdiya-kaʾʾin, the treasurer, to the land of Ituʾu (777 BC).

r 3131

r 3333

m15—BAD₃ [lu₂]GAR.KUR irina-ṣib-bi-na ina kurURI [...]

Issar-duri, the governor of Naṣibina, to the land Urarṭu [...] (774 BC).

r 3434

mman-nu—ki—d⸢IŠKUR⸣ [lu₂GAR].KUR irira-x-x-x ina ⸢kur⸣x [...]

Mannu-ki-Adad, the governor of the city Ra..., to the land ...[...] (773 BC).

r 3535

[Aššur]-belu-uṣur, the governor of Kalhu, to the city Hatarikka (772 BC).

r 3636

r 3737

r 3838

r 3939

r 4040

mIBILA-a-a [...]-a ina KUR

Aplaya, [...] ..., in the land (768 BC).

r 4141

mqur-di—aš+šur [...]-⸢na⸣ ina irigan₂-na-a-na

Qurdi-Aššur, [...] ..., to the city Gannanati (767 BC).

r 4242

Mušallim-Inurta, [...] Tille, to Media (766 BC).

r 4343

Inurta-mukin-niše, [...], to Hatarikka (765 BC).

(rest of reverse missing)

1 In most eponym lists , Nabu-šarru-uṣur is listed as the eponym for 786 BC , Adad-uballiṭ for 785 BC , and Marduk-šarru-uṣur for 784 BC.

Created by Greta Van Buylaere for the AHRC-funded GKAB Project, 2009 and released under the Creative Commons Attribution Share-Alike license 3.0. Please cite this page as http://oracc.org/cams/gkab/P338364
